turns.thought: [OBSERVE] At iteration 334, the swarm has converged to a single point with gbest=1.16e-05. No improvement for 200 iterations; diversity is virtually zero (9.28e-17), all velocities are zero, and fitness values are nearly identical. The swarm is completely stalled. | [DIAGNOSE] The swarm has collapsed prematurely. Although near the global optimum of the sphere function (0), the complete loss of diversity prevents any further refinement. The root cause is standard PSO's rapid convergence before precise location of the minimum. | [STRATEGIZE] To restore diversity without losing the current best solution, reset a portion of the worst particles randomly across the search bounds. This injects fresh sampling points while preserving gbest. Increasing inertia weight after reset promotes exploration and delays reconvergence, allowing the swarm to potentially converge closer to the true optimum.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The reset successfully reinitialized 9 weak particles, restoring diversity and breaking the collapse. The best cost remained at 1.16e-05 because the newly scattered particles didn't immediately discover a better solution. This is typical: the injected particles need a few iterations to explore and the increased inertia weight (1.1) promotes sustained exploration. So far, the intervention achieved its primary goal—restoring swarm diversity—though an immediate drop in cost didn't occur. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] Original diagnosis of premature convergence stands. The swarm was stuck at a suboptimal local region of zero velocity; resetting a portion of the swarm effectively re-dispersion the search.
